description: In shell, never hand-edit generated completion scripts and here-doc / templated output; change the source and rerun the generator.
body: In shell, generated completion scripts and here-doc / templated output are derived artifacts: editing them directly drifts from the generator and is overwritten on the next run. Use when a diff touches generated files, codegen inputs, or the the target shell and the coreutils flavor (GNU vs BSD vs busybox) that produces them. Do not use this to justify patching generated output by hand or skipping regeneration. Actions: locate the generator and its inputs, edit the source, rerun the generation step, then run the script under its declared shell (#!/usr/bin/env bash vs POSIX sh) so the regenerated output is what ships. Validation signals include a clean regeneration diff, a passing bats tests or a guarded dry-run with set -euo pipefail, and no manual edits left in the generated tree. Risk boundary: escalate before regenerating if the generator is pinned, vendored, or owned by another team, and roll back if regeneration changes unrelated files.
